module_map.responsibility: File metadata collection and representation. Collects per-file information from filesystem and git.
CLI argument parsing, configuration flags, block definitions for output layout.
Output rendering. Orchestrates grid/tree layout, applies blocks, colorizes, formats columns.
Color and icon theme loading. Manages color palettes and icon mappings per filetype.
Main orchestrator. Calls Meta::from_path, sorts, dispatches to display.
Per-file coloring (via lscolors) and icon resolution by filetype.
Git status caching and file status lookup (modified/staged/untracked).

data_flow.description: Calls fs::metadata(), reads all submodules (size, owner, permissions, filetype, etc.) and constructs Meta struct.
types_produced: Meta (contains Option<Size>, Option<Owner>, Option<FileType>, Option<Name>, Option<Symlink>, Option<INode>, Option<GitFileStatus>)

data_flow.description: Recursively collects metas for directory contents based on max_depth flag.
Applies configured sort order (name, size, date, etc.) to meta vec.
For each file, iterates flags.blocks.0 (Block enum variants). For each Block, reads meta field and calls render(colors). Returns Vec<String> of formatted values.
dependencies: meta (provides Size, Owner, Name, FileType, etc.)

data_flow.description: Collects Vec<String> per file from get_output, applies padding rules, fits into grid columns or tree indentation.
Calls inner_display_grid, renders Grid to string, outputs to stdout.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central renderer. Imports from meta (5), flags (8), color (3), icon (2), theme (1). Depends on all field types in Meta for get_output match arms. Called from core.rs as final output stage.

Hub for all file attributes. from_path orchestrates 15+ sub-module calls (size, owner, permissions, filetype, name, symlink, inode, links, git_status, etc.). Called from core::fetch and Meta::recurse_into. Imports all meta/* submodules.
Defines output schema. Block enum is matched in display::get_output (high fan-in). Blocks config passed to display logic. Configurable from CLI and config_file.

files_to_modify: src/meta/mod.rs (add Option<Checksum> field to Meta struct, compute in from_path)
src/meta/checksum.rs (new file: Checksum type with from_path and render methods)
src/display.rs (add match arm in get_output for Block::Checksum)
new_types: meta::Checksum { value: String, error: Option<String> } (mirrors pattern of Size, Owner, etc.)
Implements render(colors: &Colors) -> String for display in get_output
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Existing meta field pattern: (1) Create type in meta/*, (2) Add Option<T> field to Meta, (3) Populate in Meta::from_path with error handling, (4) Implement render() trait, (5) Add Block variant in flags/blocks.rs, (6) Add match arm in display::get_output calling meta.checksum.render(colors)
change_proposal.integration_point: In display::get_output, after Block::Name match arm, add: Block::Checksum => block_vec.push(match &meta.checksum { Some(cs) => cs.render(colors), None => colorize_missing("?") }). Add to default long() layout in flags/blocks.rs if desired, or let CLI flag control inclusion.
implementation_notes: Checksum computation requires streaming file read (use sha2 crate or similar). Consider lazy evaluation or caching to avoid I/O on every invocation.
Error handling: If file unreadable, set error field and render gracefully (e.g., 'ERR' colored red).
Colorization: Use existing Elem::* color enum (e.g., Elem::FileMedium or new Elem::Checksum variant in theme/color.rs).
Performance: SHA-256 adds O(file size) I/O. Consider --checksum flag in flags to opt-in, mirror pattern of --full-time or --blocks.
risks: I/O performance: Checksum computation scales with file size. Large file listings will be slow. Recommend flag-gated opt-in.
Caching: GitCache mirrors pattern; consider ChecksumCache if multiple runs needed (unlikely in typical CLI usage).
Cross-platform: Ensure sha2 or chosen hash crate works on macOS/Linux/Windows.
Parallelism: Meta::from_path is called sequentially in recurse_into loop. Rayon parallelization would help, but out of scope.
Display width: SHA-256 is 64 chars. Ensure term_grid padding rules accommodate or add truncation in display.rs (e.g., truncate_owner pattern).

notes.analysis_depth: Conducted 6 code-analyze-mcp tool calls (overview, 3x file_details, 2x symbol_focus) + 4 shell calls for supplementary validation. Did not trace every sub-function; focused on critical paths: from_path (metadata entry), get_output (block rendering), grid (display dispatch).
notes.module_relationships: Data flows: Meta::from_path (bottom, leaf) -> Core::fetch -> Core::sort -> display::grid -> terminal. Meta contains all file attributes as Option<T>. Flags controls which attributes to display (via Block enum). Theme/Color resolves visual styling. Git caches status.
notes.key_insight: lsd architecture cleanly separates concerns: (1) meta/* collects attributes, (2) flags/* defines schema (Blocks), (3) display/* renders schema to output, (4) theme/* supplies styling. Adding a new column requires: meta type + Option field + render impl + Block variant + get_output match arm. Very modular.
notes.code_quality: Well-structured Rust with error handling patterns, read-only file operations, no unsafe detected in high-level modules. Uses standard patterns: Option<T> for optional fields, trait-based render(), enum for extensible Block types.
